channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Las Vegas hotel workers union reaches deal with casino to end longest strike in decades
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — The longest strike in decades by Las Vegas hotel workers ended Wednesday with a ratified contract between the union and a casino where hundreds of employees had walked off the job in November.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democratic infighting is rising as Trump takes center stage
item.description: Democrats are finding themselves mired in infighting and schoolyard sniping just as President Trump begins his new term. Former first lady Jill Biden and Rep. Nancy Pelosi’s (D-Calif.
item.dc:creator: Amie Parnes, NewsNation

item.title: 20,000 ordered to evacuate as fast-moving wildfire imperils homes north of Los Angeles
item.description: CASTAIC, Calif. (AP) — Nearly 20,000 people were ordered to evacuate on Wednesday as a huge and fast-moving wildfire swept through rugged mountains north of Los Angeles, as parched Southern California endured another round of dangerous winds and two major previous blazes continued to smolder.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER and MARCIO JOSE SANCHEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mistrial declared after New Hampshire jury deadlocks in youth detention center rape case
item.description: CONCORD, N.H. (AP) — A judge declared a mistrial Wednesday after jurors deadlocked on whether a former worker at New Hampshire's youth detention center raped a 14-year-old boy in 1998, but the defendant still faces 15 other charges in separate cases.
item.dc:creator: HOLLY RAMER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Idaho murders: Judge in Bryan Kohberger case hears defense motion
item.description: Idaho murders suspect Bryan Kohberger is back in court for pre-trial hearings ahead of his summer murder trial for the brutal stabbing deaths of four University of Idaho students.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fire chief shot, killed after stopping to help driver who struck a deer in Alabama
item.description: “We lost one of our brothers, leaders, mentor, and family to a senseless tragedy,” Cauthen's colleagues wrote in a Facebook post.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth White, ABC 4

item.title: Wisconsin man accused of setting fire to congressman's office over TikTok ban charged with arson
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Prosecutors charged a Wisconsin man who allegedly told police he tried to burn down a congressman's office because he was upset with the federal TikTok ban with multiple counts on Wednesday, including arson.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump popularity higher than throughout most of first term: Poll
item.description: The poll found Trump with a 47 percent approval rating, higher than almost the entirety of his first term.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, NewsNation

item.title: Man who got Jan. 6 pardon is arrested on federal gun charge
item.description: The Florida man appears to be the first Jan. 6 rioter to face additional legal trouble after the sweeping clemency bestowed on those who rioted at the Capitol.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WGN 9

item.title: Trump pardoned violent Jan. 6 rioters. Here are 5 of them
item.description: Hundreds of rioters accused of violently assaulting police at the Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021, were pardoned Monday by President Trump, despite remarks from his allies in recent weeks condemning those defendants.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: GOP leaders stare down legislative minefield after Trump's Day 1 blitz
item.description: President Trump made a big splash with dozens of executive actions on his first day in office, but now comes the hard part: getting Republicans in Congress in line to usher an ambitious legislative agenda through their slim majorities and clashing factions. Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration shuts down White House Spanish-language page and social media
item.description: Within hours of President Donald Trump’s inauguration, the new administration took down the Spanish-language version of the official White House website. The site — currently https://www.whitehouse.gov/es/ — now gives users an “Error 404” message.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump popularity higher than throughout most of first term: Poll
item.description: The poll found Trump with a 47 percent approval rating, higher than almost the entirety of his first term, though still below the majority support that most other presidents have enjoyed at the start of their administrations.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, KRON4

item.title: Justice Department directs prosecutors to investigate local efforts to obstruct immigration enforcement
item.description: The Justice Department is directing its federal prosecutors to investigate for potential criminal charges any state or local officials who stand in the way of beefed-up enforcement of immigration laws under the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Woman beaten with brass knuckles during Memphis robbery
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. -- A woman is recovering from injuries to her eye and mouth after she was punched and stomped on during a robbery outside a Walgreens in the Midtown area of Memphis, Tennessee. The victim told police she exited the store in the 1800 block of Union Avenue around 10:15 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Moon, NewsNation

item.title: DOJ to investigate state or local officials who obstruct immigration enforcement: memo
item.description: A new DOJ memo signals that federal prosecutors should launch investigations into state and local officials who obstruct immigration enforcement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: California water supply crucial for LA wildfire response allowed to run dry months before infernos: lawsuit
item.description: Over a dozen victims of the Pacific Palisades Fire are suing a Los Angeles public utility company for "failures," which contributed to the fire's devastation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Adams says NYC is coordinating with ICE as mass migrant deportations loom
item.description: New York City Mayor Eric Adams said his administration has been coordinating with ICE with regard to deporting criminal illegal migrants.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 5 takeaways on Trump's executive actions on gender
item.description: President Donald Trump signed a sweeping executive order late Monday during his first hours in office recognizing only two sexes and preventing government dollars from being spent on what his administration called “gender ideology.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KRON4

item.title: Mittens the cat becomes an accidental frequent flyer after getting mistakenly left on a plane
item.description: A Maine coon cat named Mittens became an accidental jetsetter this month when her cage was overlooked in a plane cargo hold and she made three trips in 24 hours between New Zealand and Australia.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Texas mother charged after found lying outside in freezing temps with two babies: sheriff's office
item.description: The Harris County Sheriff's Office responded to a 911 call about a woman lying on a sidewalk with her two babies in what weather forecasters described as historic snowfall.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China and US partners are moving closer as Trump returns to the White House
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — China's relations are starting to improve with Japan, India and other countries that former U.S. President Joe Biden courted, just as Donald Trump brings his more unilateralist approach back to the White House.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU and DIDI TANG, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Kentucky sheriff accused of killing judge makes court appearance from jail
item.description: A former Kentucky sheriff accused of gunning down a judge last year, stunning their tight-knit Appalachian community, appeared remotely from a jail Friday for a court hearing as defense attorneys and prosecutors discussed preliminary matters that will lead up to his murder trial.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Guitarist John Sykes of Whitesnake, Thin Lizzy dies
item.description: "In his final days, he spoke of his sincere love and gratitude for his fans who stuck by him through all these years," a statement released Monday read.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: US border patrol agent was fatally shot in Vermont near Canada
item.description: Authorities on Tuesday were investigating a fatal shooting of a U.S. Border Patrol agent that also left a suspect dead and another injured on a Vermont highway near Canada, authorities said. Agent David Maland was killed Monday afternoon following a traffic stop, a U.S.
item.dc:creator: KATHY McCORMACK and PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Illinois among 22 states suing to stop Trump's order blocking birthright citizenship
item.description: CHICAGO — Illinois Attorney General Kwame Raoul on Tuesday joined three other states in a lawsuit challenging President Donald Trump's executive order that seeks to end birthright citizenship.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump's first day back in White House includes firings, infrastructure announcement
item.description: President Donald Trump is spending his first full day back in the White House meeting with congressional leaders, making an infrastructure announcement and demonstrating one of his favored expressions of power: firing people.
item.dc:creator: Michelle L. Price, ABC 4

item.title: American deputy killed by stray bullet on Turks and Caicos vacation
item.description: Illinois sheriff's deputy Shamone Duncan was killed alongside a local resident while on vacation in Turks and Caicos for her sister's birthday this weekend.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nose spray to treat depression? FDA approves first-of-its-kind medicine
item.description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration has approved a nose spray to help treat depression, Johnson & Johnson announced Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, WGN 9

item.title: Why Richard Allen's attorneys say conviction should be vacated in Delphi murders case
item.description: DELPHI, Ind. – Attorneys for convicted Delphi murderer Richard Allen filed a flurry of documents this week suggesting their client’s murder conviction should be vacated.
item.dc:creator: Matt Adams, WGN 9

item.title: Border Patrol agent killed in Vermont
item.description: A U.S. Border Patrol agent was shot and killed in northern Vermont on Monday afternoon, the FBI said in a statement later that evening.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WGN 9

item.title: A rare winter storm is bringing heavy snow, sleet and ice to Texas and the northern Gulf Coast
item.description: A rare winter storm charging through Texas and the northern Gulf Coast on Tuesday has closed highways and airports and prompted the first blizzard warning for southeast Texas and southwest Louisiana.
item.dc:creator: SARAH BRUMFIELD Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Here are the biggest fights Democrats are bracing for in Trump's second term
item.description: House Democrats are bracing for major fights in the coming year as President Trump enters the White House for his second term and Republicans control all levers of power in Washington.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, Fox 5

item.title: Fire crews in Southern California quickly extinguish brush fires amid extreme fire weather
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Firefighters quickly extinguished several brush fires that erupted Monday in Southern California amid windy and dry conditions.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Costco workers authorize nationwide strike
item.description: Thousands of Costco workers nationwide voted Sunday to authorize a strike if a contract is not reached by the end of the month.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, Fox 5

item.title: WATCH: Carrie Underwood sings 'America the Beautiful' a cappella after apparent tech issue at inauguration
item.description: An apparent technical issue didn't stop Carrie Underwood from delivering "America the Beautiful" during the second inauguration of President Trump on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KTLA 5

item.title: Family of Utah student who died hiking in Hawaii over winter break speaks out
item.description: "I know her goals in life were to protect lands and promote a healthy place to live for all of us. I can just speak in volumes to what an amazing, beautiful, strong woman she is.
item.dc:creator: Jenn Boneza, ABC 4
